{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Scandinavian Defence| |rd|nd|bd| |kd|bd|nd|rd|= |pd|pd|pd| |pd|pd|pd|pd|= | | | |qd| | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= | | |nl| | | | | |= |pl|pl|pl|pl| |pl|pl|pl|= |rl| |bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= || }} = Scandinavian Defense: Mieses-Kotrč, Gubinsky-Melts Defense = Rather than moving the queen to d8 or a5 after Nc3, the Gubinsky-Melts Defense opts to keep the queen in the center of the board, controlling many important squares, but allowing the queen to be a more ideal target compared to the queen going all the way back to d8 or a5, where it is tucked into a safer square. ==Theory table== {{ChessTable}}. 1. e4 d5 2. exd5 Qxd5 3.Nc3 Qd6
